Off Calendar Jun-23-2022 Set For Trial On Feb-21-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jun-20-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Byron Sun, Esq. (415.767.0630; byron.sun@bsunlaw.com) for Plaintiffs (audio only).
Sean Hanle, Esq. (310.855.3000; shanle@willkie.com) of Willkie Farr & Gallagher LLP for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED re: voluntary substitution of attorney form to be filed or a motion to be relieved as counsel to be filed by today on behalf of plaintiffs.
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS VALERIE HARLAN and CHRISTOPHER HARLAN's Motion For Protective Order.

The Motion is granted. Plaintiffs shall have 20 days to respond to the outstanding discovery.

No sanctions.

Mr. Sun to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
